name: bZx(0215)
report: The attacker exploited a logical flaw in bZx protocol's `marginTradeFromDeposit()` function (within its internal call chain). The flaw caused the contract to skip a critical risk validation check (`shouldLiquidate()`) that was designed to prevent unhealthy leveraged positions. This allowed the attacker to use a flash loan and 5x leverage to manipulate the WBTC price on Uniswap via KyberSwap, creating an artificial price spike. The attacker then profited by dumping pre-borrowed WBTC at the inflated price, ultimately netting approximately 1,271 ETH (worth ~$355,880 at the time).
